strategies.name: Gate Workflow Composition Fingerprinting
strategies.finding: 1 gate=trivial, 4-7=moderate, 9+=feature. Security Review Agent co-occurrence=high-risk. Haiku-Printer-only=auto-merge candidate

strategies.name: Gate Sweep Branch Concentration
strategies.finding: Branch dominating >40% of daily sessions = routine/templated workflow with tight duration variance

strategies.name: Inverse Gate-Count to Conclusiveness
strategies.finding: Per-branch gate count strongly inversely correlated with conclusive rate. >15 gates/hour = waiting on agent action, not green CI

strategies.name: Success-Workflow Provenance Mapping
strategies.finding: On gate-sweep days 100% of successes originate from the Running-Copilot-cloud-agent workflow; gate workflows (Q/Smoke CI/CJS/CGO/Agentic Commands/moderators) never produce success conclusions (only action_required). Completion-rate denominator is dominated by gate firings, so daily completion% mostly tracks gate-to-agent-session ratio, not agent quality.

strategies.name: Success-Workflow Provenance Inversion
strategies.finding: On recovery days (completion>=40%) gate/moderator/review workflows (Test Quality Sentinel, PR Code Quality Reviewer, Design Decision Gate, Matt Pocock, Code Review, Agentic Commands) DO produce success conclusions -- 17/20 successes vs only 3 from cloud-agent. Inverts the 06-03 provenance mapping which held only on pure gate-sweep days. Implication: completion% is bimodal by regime, not purely a gate-to-agent ratio.
